decision_row: m13 CLOSE — P-SLIDE V-B (contact-only, two vertical rails, gravity along travel)
g9_note: G-CONV (1 s at-rest equilibrium) is EXPECTED to fail: under gravity-along-travel the bare slide has no rest state without the pawl — consistent with the P-HOLD finding, not a numerical defect.

CHECKPOINT.verdict: V-B RETENTION FAILS at the frozen preset — RECORDED, NOT TUNED (the brief's rule)
CHECKPOINT.interface: carriage-lip ↔ rail-head T-groove retention (the slide_rail mechanism contact class)
CHECKPOINT.scale: contacts lost at t≈0.02 s (ncon 8→0); peak off-axis 180.0°, peak lateral drift 116.1 mm — the free platform escapes the groove
CHECKPOINT.diagnosis: the T-rail is a CLEARANCE fit whose retention was gravity-SEATED in the horizontal drawer (m10 V-B 5/5). Rotated vertical, gravity acts ALONG travel and no longer presses the carriage into the groove, so the clearance-fit lips are not engaged; any off-centre load (the rack/pinion COM offset) pitches the platform out. This is the V-A/V-B distinction: the declared prismatic joint (V-A) enforced retention 5/5; the contact geometry alone does not, under gravity-along-travel.
CHECKPOINT.design_implication: a vertical contact slide needs a PRELOADED / near-zero-clearance retention (or the pawl + a bottom stop), not the drawer's gravity-seated fit — a finding the horizontal m10 could not surface. A design change, deferred; the preset is untouched.
